head: cf0c6e6 2026-07-14 16:17:17 +0200
branches_note: origin/main (07da4cb) is 1 ahead of dev but only the PR#29 merge of dev — no unique content. origin/feat/m2-e2e-gate-harness (0f8eefb) is 1 ahead / 3 behind dev, carrying the M2 E2E closing-gate harness (tests/e2e/gate_m2_e2e_test.go, ENG-1574) not yet on dev. origin/crew/daniel is stale (37 behind). No ENG-2069 hazard: no dev branch is meaningfully ahead of the audited ref.

E1-S1.note: multi-issuer verifier maps Clerk + Keycloak tokens to one gen.Principal (deny-by-default, fail-closed); real Clerk/Keycloak resolvers.

E1-S2.note: verifier.go delegates the full crypto pipeline to orvex-studio-lib/pkg/auth MultiIssuerVerifier; deny-by-default Middleware + RequireScope.

E1-S3.note: POST /v1/introspect + dual-path Introspector (Clerk session OR opaque grant) present with tests; MISSING the Redis-cached introspection cadence/TTL — cache.go ships only the RevocationDenyList as real Redis, the introspection cache is still stdlib scaffold.

E1-S4.note: fresh-introspect gate (fail-closed when the deny-list is unreachable for a high-privilege call) + real Redis push-revocation deny-list + Broadcast-on-revoke present; MISSING the fixed grace hard-bound max-staleness (tied to the still-scaffolded introspection cache).

E2-S1.note: Mint freezes claims onto the grant row and introspection reconstructs the principal from that frozen row; MISSING the org-switch immutability guarantee — req.Tenant org-switch re-exchange is explicitly NOT honored (server.go:935) and there is no org-A-vs-org-B CI test.

E2-S2.note: POST /v1/tokens is gated by requireSession (bearer) and mints via token.Authority; MISSING explicit CSRF/origin binding + first-party proof-of-user for the browser front ends.

E2-S3.note: scope derived server-side (derivePlatformScope + intersectWithTokenScope, never client input) and enforced via RequireScope (lib/auth, deny-by-default).

E2-S4.note: List + Revoke with the revocation event written to the transactional outbox in one txn (RevokeAndEnqueue) and a within-TTL revocation integration test; MISSING rotate (no rotate handler exists).

E3-S1.note: polymorphic {user|org} tenancy (TenantKey) over org_workspaces; opaque, non-derivable Subject/Tenant ids; registry-backed.

E3-S2.note: OIDC JIT provisioning through OidcUserProvisioner + fixed-window ThrottleLimiters (ported 30/60-per-window limits) + Clerk JIT with concurrent-race idempotency.

E3-S3.note: polymorphic personal/org tenancy + ReactivateMembers + an org-convert journal event are referenced; MISSING the actual Personal->Teams upgrade pass (org mint + tenant re-key) and access-transition isolation — org-switch re-exchange is not honored.

E3-S4.note: no Clerk org_role -> workspace role enum mapping anywhere in the tree.

E4-S1.note: POST /v1/exchange verifies the Clerk session (clerk.Resolver.Verify) then mints a short-lived opaque exchange token via the same token.Authority; engine consumes it (never sets the cookie itself).

E4-S2.note: identity-side native-login lockout is real (ErrSSORequired + isFederated + PUT enforce-sso admin route); the actual removal of the engine's password login/change/forgot/reset + /setup endpoints lives in the engine repo, not here.

E4-S3.note: offline-sealed ed25519 break-glass credential mint+verify + sealtool CLI; fail-closed verify (expired/bad-sig/wrong-aud distinguished for logging only).

E4-S4.note: full Keycloak OIDC-RP relocated as native Go (authorize/callback/provider CRUD/mirror/discovery-cache/PKCE); no @docmost imports (Go repo).

E4-S5.note: SCIM 2.0 Users/Groups CRUD + membership PATCH + SCIM bearer-token mgmt behind a timing-safe HMAC guard; NOTE the stores are in-memory reference impls (MemoryUserStore/etc), a Postgres-backed impl is deferred.

E4-S6.note: admin routes relocated (setEnforceSSO/clerkConfig/oidcProviders) behind tenant-scoped authz + restricted-token ceiling; MISSING a dedicated admin-org authz gate (today it is self-tenant scoping only).

E5-S1.note: provision/deprovision REST handlers exist (POST /api/clerk/provision + /deprovision, provisionEnginePrincipal/provisionAIWorkspace); MISSING workload identity (mTLS/SPIFFE) + NetworkPolicy — referenced only as upstream/deployment concerns, no NetworkPolicy manifest in deploy/.

E5-S2.note: the deprovision REST route exists and a cmd/deprovisioner Knative worker is wired; MISSING the two-phase soft-disable + grace-gated purge behaviour — the worker's Handle only decodes + logs a TODO, no workflow dispatch/purge.

E5-S3.note: transactional outbox (Outbox port + store/postgres/outbox.go, written atomically by registry + token revoke) and CloudEvents Consumer/envelope decode present; MISSING the outbound relay — event.Relay.Publish returns "not implemented" (no Kafka producer yet).

E5-S4.note: the reconciler CloudEvent worker + orphan-sweep are implemented (real Tenancy-backed SpineReconciler); MISSING the F2 resolution itself — retiring the CloudEvent worker in favour of a REST-callee-only path is still flagged an OPEN decision in-code.

E6-S1.note: PII-free routing core is a real schema-confined store (RegistryCore over registry_tenant_cells/registry_hostnames) with isolation tests; MISSING the sensitive in-jurisdiction shard as a separate schema-isolated store (stripe_customer/contacts not implemented).

E6-S2.note: Assign picks the home cell at creation by geo-proximity + residency pin and ReserveHostname enforces global hostname/slug uniqueness; idempotent re-assign.

E6-S3.note: the routing-core Resolve read path + a 2nd-cell routes-only replication prep (internal/replicate DryRun) exist; MISSING the Workers KV mirror + Cloudflare edge geo-router read path (no Cloudflare/KV code).

E6-S4.note: registry.Move (the activate step) + move_consumer + a Kafka tenant-move trigger + an ORG_MOVE.md store inventory exist; MISSING the full typed quiesce/export/import step API + TenantMoveManifest (token_grants/org_workspaces are noted uncovered; the file is ORG_MOVE.md, not TENANT_MOVE.md).

E7-S1.note: GET /v1/whoami mounted behind requireSession + cellPinGuard, echoing the verified principal (opaque-bearer path covered).

E7-S2.note: no device-authorization / token-grant flow for `orvex auth login` — clerk/magic.go is a browser-iframe magic-link/OTP exchange, not a CLI device-code flow.

E7-S3.note: GET /healthz (cell-contract echo) exists and the engine client carries an INTERNAL_API_BEARER_TOKEN; MISSING the /healthz doctor probe alias and a documented interim engine api-key passthrough surface.

E8-S1.note: JSONB append journal (EventJournal) + real Redis (RevocationDenyList) + tenant-scoped isolation asserted by grants/entitlements integration tests; MISSING DB-level Postgres RLS policies — isolation is application-tier "RLS-by-tenant" query scoping, no CREATE POLICY / ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY.

E8-S2.note: /healthz echoes CELL_ID + CLUSTER_NAME; MISSING the livez/readyz split (both probes hit /healthz) and CELL_ID is not strictly required (defaults to the "solo" sentinel).

E8-S3.note: cellPinGuard 421 (StatusMisdirectedRequest) self-heal middleware present with an integration test; MISSING an HTTP Idempotency-Key on the internal step APIs (registry has a move-id ledger, not the request header).

E8-S4.note: audit stream is real (EventJournal + outbox events on mint/revoke/cell-assign); MISSING OTel/Prometheus — no /metrics endpoint (observability.yaml admits this) and no otel instrumentation in code.

E8-S5.note: A-TOKEN/A-VERIFY/A-SESSION are captured in the service architecture doc under _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture; MISSING dedicated standalone ADR files authored per SE-Arch §7.

E9-S1.note: no verified_principal entity and no principal-scoped lifetime AI-trial ledger anywhere in the tree.

E9-S2.note: no per-principal live-tenant cap, progressive-trust tiers, or staged provisioning.

E9-S3.note: no table-stakes edge controls — no Turnstile, disposable-email block, IP/ASN, or velocity checks.

E9-S4.note: no signup risk-score fusion / ALLOW-STEP-UP-DENY logic or risk-triggered step-up.

E10-S1.note: no BYOK / KMS customer-managed-key path (store-layer or otherwise).

E10-S2.note: no SOC 2 Type II / HIPAA BAA compliance-path artifacts in the repo.

E10-S3.note: residency primitives exist (residency_pin + residencyCells steer cell selection at Assign); MISSING the self-host / BYOC data-residency option itself.

E10-S4.note: no personal<->employer firewall, per-use consent, or AD-8 mint-boundary delivery token.

summary: orvex-studio-identity is a mature, heavily-tested Go service: the verification spine (E1), token authority (E2), tenant/JIT spine (E3), exchange + Keycloak-OIDC + SCIM + break-glass seams (E4), and the global cell registry (E6) are largely real with meaningful integration tests. The consistent gaps are the not-yet-wired async/edge pieces (Redis introspection cache, outbox->Kafka relay, deprovision two-phase worker, Workers-KV edge router) and, wholesale, the anti-Sybil epic (E9) and most of the memory-security/compliance epic (E10 BYOK/SOC2/BAA/consent), which are absent. 11 present, 22 partial, 9 absent of 42 stories.
